zh.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY是Jeanne de CALLETOT的兒子，出生。他和Péronnelle de VILLIERS至少有1個兒子。他去世於1385年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY是Jeanne de CALLETOT的儿子，出生。他和Péronnelle de VILLIERS至少有1个儿子。他去世于1385年。

nameMeanings.name: de

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Charles
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Germanic word, karl, meaning ‘free man’, akin to Old English ceorl ‘man’. The name, Latin form Carolus, owed its popularity in medieval Europe to the Frankish leader Charlemagne ( ?742–814 ), who in 800 established himself as Holy Roman Emperor. His name (Latin Carolus Magnus) means ‘Charles the Great’. Carolus—or Karl, the German form—was a common name among Frankish leaders, including Charlemagne's grandfather Charles Martel ( 688–741 ). Charles is the French form. The name occurs occasionally in medieval Britain as Karolus or Carolus; it had a certain vogue in West Yorkshire from the 1400s, particularly among gentry families. The form Charles was chosen by Mary Queen of Scots ( 1542–87 ), who had been brought up in France, for her son, Charles James ( 1566–1625 ), who became King James VI of Scotland and, from 1603 , James I of England. His son and grandson both reigned as King Charles , and the name thus became established in the 17th century both in the Stuart royal house and among English and Scottish supporters of the Stuart monarchy. In the 18th century it was to some extent favoured, along with James , by Jacobites, supporters of the exiled Stuarts, opposed to the Hanoverian monarchy, especially in the Highlands of Scotland. In the 19th century the popularity of the name was further enhanced by romanticization of the story of ‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’, leader of the 1745 rebellion.

en.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY was born as the son of Jeanne de CALLETOT. He had at least 1 son with Péronnelle de VILLIERS. He died in 1385.

fr.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY est né, fils de Jeanne de CALLETOT. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Péronnelle de VILLIERS. Il est décédé en 1385.

de.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY wurde, als Sohn von Jeanne de CALLETOT geboren. Er hatte mit Péronnelle de VILLIERS mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1385.

it.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY è nato il come figlio di Jeanne de CALLETOT. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Péronnelle de VILLIERS. Morì nel 1385.

ja.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCYはJeanne de CALLETOTの息子として生まれた。彼はPéronnelle de VILLIERSとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1385年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY(은)는 Jeanne de CALLETOT의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Péronnelle de VILLIERS(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1385년 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY nasceu filho de Jeanne de CALLETOT. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Péronnelle de VILLIERS. Ele faleceu em 1385.

ru.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY родился. Его мать звали Jeanne de CALLETOT. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Péronnelle de VILLIERS. Он умер в 1385 г.

es.lifeSummary: Charles de MONTMORENCY nació como hijo de Jeanne de CALLETOT. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Péronnelle de VILLIERS. Murió en 1385.
